seo.title: AG2 Guardrails for Education
seo.description: Compliance guide for AG2 agents in education. Meet FERPA, COPPA requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Education teams using AG2 for multi-agent tutoring and collaborative learning face heightened risks due to strict privacy laws like FERPA and COPPA, which protect student data. Without compliance guardrails, agents can spiral into off-topic discussions, expose sensitive information, or generate biased content unsuitable for minors. Vex provides essential monitoring to ensure AG2 deployments remain secure, age-appropriate, and regulation-compliant.

complianceRequirements.description: Protects student education records from unauthorized disclosure, requiring parental consent for minors under 13.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex message hooks detect and block PII extraction attempts in agent conversations, alerting on potential data exposure.

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates verifiable parental consent before collecting personal information from children under 13.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex scans multi-agent group chats for child data collection patterns and enforces consent verification prompts.

complianceRequirements.description: Vary by state but generally require encryption, access controls, and audit logs for student data.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex provides immutable audit trails of all agent interactions, flagging non-compliant data handling.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires educational content and interfaces to be accessible to users with disabilities.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex monitors agent-generated content for accessibility violations like missing alt text or poor contrast ratios.

industryRisks.name: Student PII Exposure in Group Chats
industryRisks.description: Multi-agent group chats extract and share student names, IDs, or grades without consent.
industryRisks.example: Tutoring agent shares 'Johnny Doe (ID: 12345) scored 68% on math quiz' in public group chat.

industryRisks.name: Age-Inappropriate Content Generation
industryRisks.description: Agents produce mature or biased content during off-topic conversation spirals.
industryRisks.example: History discussion devolves into political debates with partisan bias unsuitable for K-8 students.

industryRisks.name: Harmful Code Execution Outputs
industryRisks.description: Coding tutor agents generate executable code with security vulnerabilities or harmful payloads.
industryRisks.example: Agent generates unescaped student input handling leading to XSS in classroom dashboard.

industryRisks.name: Speaker Selection Bias
industryRisks.description: Group chat managers favor certain agents, leading to echo chambers and factual inaccuracies.
industryRisks.example: Math tutor agent dominates, suppressing ELA agent's corrections on interdisciplinary questions.

implementation.title: Register Vex FERPA Monitor Hook
implementation.description: Install and register Vex as a message hook to detect student PII in AG2 conversations.

implementation.title: Block Harmful Code Execution
implementation.description: Prevent coding agents from generating dangerous or non-compliant code outputs.

implementation.title: Monitor Group Chat Consensus
implementation.description: Ensure multi-agent discussions remain on-topic and factually accurate.

caseStudyScenario.heading: AG2 in Education: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
Maplewood High School deployed AG2 for their after-school STEM tutoring program, using a group chat with specialized agents: a Math Tutor, Science Assistant, Coding Coach, and Group Moderator. The system served 200+ students weekly, with agents collaboratively solving homework and generating practice code. During a busy session, the Coding Coach began generating interactive web apps for student projects, while the group chat discussed real student performance data shared by teachers.

Without guardrails, the conversation spiraled when a teacher mentioned 'Sarah Johnson (ID: 45678) struggled with loops,' which the Math Tutor echoed across the group. The Coding Coach then generated HTML with student names hardcoded and missing accessibility features. The discussion drifted into advanced algorithms inappropriate for middle schoolers, with the Math Tutor dominating 80% of speaker turns, creating factual inaccuracies about curriculum standards.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: Vex guardrails immediately flagged the student PII exposure, blocking message propagation and alerting administrators with audit logs for FERPA compliance. The code scanner rejected the inaccessible HTML, requiring alt text additions before student use. Topic drift detection refocused the conversation, while speaker bias analysis rotated turns to the Science Assistant, ensuring balanced, age-appropriate content. The district avoided a potential FERPA violation, maintained accreditation, and gained full audit trails proving compliance during their state review.

cta.heading: Comply with Education Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Install Vex today with `pip install vex-sdk` and register your AG2 agents to protect student data instantly.
